history.content:
<thought>
The goal is visible up ahead to the right, but the cell directly in front of me contains lava, so moving forward would be unsafe. The open safe path is to my right, so I should rotate clockwise first.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
The goal is visible ahead-left relative to my current position, but the cell directly in front of me is lava, so moving forward would be unsafe. Turning left would face a wall, while turning right would face an open floor cell. I should turn right to align with the safe path toward the goal.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
The cell directly in front of me is free floor, while turning would not immediately improve my position. Moving forward is safe and continues along the open corridor.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>
I am facing upward, and the cell directly in front of me is an empty floor cell, so moving forward is safe. Turning is unnecessary because there is no visible goal yet and forward progress explores open space.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>
I am facing upward with a wall directly in front of me. The open floor is to my left and right on my current row, but I cannot move there without turning. I will turn left to face a safe open cell.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_LEFT
</action>

<thought>
The goal is visible to my upper-left, but I am currently facing upward and the cell directly in front of me is a safe floor cell. Turning toward the goal immediately would face lava nearby and a wall blocks the right side. Advancing forward into the open corridor is the safest progress.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>
The goal is visible to my left, and the cell directly in front of me is a wall, so moving forward is blocked. The cell to my left is open floor and leads toward the goal, so I should turn left to face that safe path.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_LEFT
</action>

<thought>
The goal is visible ahead and slightly to the right, but I am currently facing upward. The cell directly in front of me is safe floor, while turning is unnecessary right now. Moving forward advances me toward the row containing the goal and avoids the lava to my left.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>
The goal is visible ahead and to the right, but the cell directly in front of me contains lava, so moving forward is unsafe. Both left and right adjacent cells appear to be lava on the current row, but turning is safe and necessary to seek another route. I will turn right to reorient toward the open corridor near the goal.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
The goal is visible one row ahead and one cell to the left, but I am facing upward and the cell directly in front of me is floor. To reach the goal safely, I should first move forward into the open cell, which will put me adjacent to the goal without stepping onto lava.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>
